feedback_eng: The ecological status of this freshwater cannot be determined because it is currently dry. Some waterbodies naturally appear and disappear, with these known as ephemeral habitats. These can be extremely biodiverse and important habitats for groups such as amphibians. However, some habitats may be drying up due to climate change and unfavourable environmental conditions - this is concerning. Monitoring helps us to understand where and how often this is happening. Your FreshWater Watch survey helps us to understand which freshwaters are most vulnerable to climate change.

feedback_eng: The ecological status of this freshwater cannot be determined because it is currently dry. Some waterbodies naturally appear and disappear, with these known as ephemeral habitats. These can be extremely biodiverse and important habitats for groups such as amphibians. However, some habitats may be drying up due to climate change and unfavourable environmental conditions - this is concerning. Monitoring helps us to understand where and how often this is happening. Your FreshWater Watch survey helps us to understand which freshwaters are most vulnerable to climate change.

These values are expected to vary throughout the year as a result of weather patterns and changes in land use. Please help us to better understand this ecosystem by continuing to make measurements.
notes: This pipe is an important contributor of water volume to the headwaters of this stream.  I only noticed it earlier this year.  My guess - and it is a guess - given the relatively good water quality is that it is picking up ground and surface water from the spring line and roads around Spring Street in Chipping Norton.
